title: UI Manager service for handling blocking and non-blocking UI renders
purpose: Manages UI rendering requests and responses for both blocking and non-blocking modes, supporting session-based caching and promise resolution.
module_context: Exports a UIManager class and a singleton instance for managing UI state and responses across sessions.

steps.content: Generate a timestamp-based component for the UI ID.
Generate a random 6-character hexadecimal component for the UI ID.
Combine the timestamp and random components into a unique UI ID string.
Validate that project and session are provided in the request.
Validate that UI is a non-null object with a type property.
Normalize the blocking flag to default to true if not provided.
Use the provided UI ID or generate a new one if not provided.
Create a session key from project and session.
Cache the UI in the session map for reconnection recovery.
IF the mode is non-blocking, return an immediate terminal response.
ELSE create and return a promise that will resolve when a response is received.

methods.name: receiveResponse
methods.normalized_params: (sessionKey: string, uiId: string, response: Partial<UIResponse>): boolean

steps.content: Look up the cached UI for the given session key and UI ID.
IF no cached UI is found or the UI ID does not match, return false.
Update the cached UI status to responded and record the response time.
IF a pending UI exists for the session, resolve the promise with the response.
IF a pending UI exists, clean up the pending UI state.
Return true to indicate successful response handling.
Look up the pending UI for the given session key.
IF no pending UI is found, return false.
Update the cached UI status to canceled.
Reject the pending promise with a dismissal error.
Clean up the pending UI state from the map.
Return true to indicate successful dismissal.

methods.name: getUIStatus
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, uiId: string): { status: 'pending' | 'responded' | 'stale' | 'not_found'; action?: string; data?: Record<string, any> }

steps.content: Construct a session key from project and session.
Look up the cached UI for the session key.
IF no cached UI is found, return not_found status.
IF the UI ID does not match the cached UI, return stale status.
IF the cached UI status is responded, return responded with action and data.
ELSE return pending status.
